free instant test

Reaction Time Test

When the lights go out, tap. That’s the whole test — and it measures you to the millisecond, the same way Formula 1 measures race starts. Most people land around 250ms on a visual cue; trained athletes and top gamers live below 200. Touchscreens add a little latency, so compare against yourself on the same device.

▶  START THE TEST
Average adult (visual)~250 ms
Excellentunder 200 ms
F1 driver territory~150–200 ms
Your scoreone tap away

Questions

What is the average reaction time?

Around 250 milliseconds for a visual stimulus. Under 200ms is excellent. Audio cues are typically 20-40ms faster than visual ones.

Is this reaction time test free?

Yes — it runs instantly in your browser with no download or sign-up, and you can retry as many times as you like.

Why is my reaction time slower on my phone?

Touchscreens add input latency compared to a mouse or keyboard. For a fair benchmark, compare runs on the same device.
